big_picture:
Excise Duty Cut on Petrol and Diesel: A Bold Governance Move Amid War has been implemented by the government, reducing the burden on consumers amidst the West Asian conflict. This move has led to a fall in stock markets by 1% and the rupee has fallen past 94/$. The government has explained that the decision to cut excise duty on petrol and diesel was taken to mitigate the impact of the conflict on the economy. Fuel Panic vs Reality: What the Data Says About India's Petrol Supply suggests that the country's petrol supply is not as affected as feared. The government's people-centric governance has been highlighted by the fuel excise duty cuts amid price rise globally, according to Union Home Minister Shah.

The West Asian conflict has upended global monetary policy, raising concerns over urea supply in India. The conflict has put pressure on the rupee, inflation, and GDP growth, fueling oil and inflation risks. The Prime Minister is set to meet with Chief Ministers amid the Iran escalation.

Parliament has passed a bill to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act 2019, providing for graded punishment based on the gravity of harm inflicted on transgender persons. The bill also seeks to amend the existing law to better protect the rights of transgender individuals. The bill was passed despite a fiery standoff in the Rajya Sabha led by Jaya Bachchan.

story_clusters.headline: Excise Duty Cut on Petrol and Diesel: A Bold Governance Move Amid War
story_clusters.summary: India has reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel to offset rising global crude prices due to the geopolitical situation in West Asia. This move is in response to the impact of the Iran war on crude oil prices, which has led to a fall in the rupee value and a decline in stock markets. The government aimed to mitigate the effects of the price increase on consumers.

Foreign.summary:
The Union Government in India has notified the provisions of the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, aimed at regulating sports governance in the country. 

US President Trump has paused strikes on energy sites in the Middle East and has agreed to meet with Iran for peace talks in Pakistan, with the deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz extended to 6 April.

The Union Government has notified the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, primarily to promote the national song "Vande Mataram" in sports events. 

A plea has been filed in the Karnataka High Court against a Ministry of Home Affairs circular that allegedly imposes a "religiously loaded" version of "Vande Mataram" on students. 

The Allahabad High Court's 1971 judgment did not declare the Ramayana a fictional story.

Governance.summary:
The Union Government has notified the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, primarily to promote grassroots sports development. 

The Karnataka High Court has agreed to hear a plea challenging the Ministry of Home Affairs' protocol over singing a "religiously loaded" version of the national song, Vande Mataram, in educational institutions. 

The government has reduced the excise duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre and exempted diesel from it to help oil marketing companies.

The Indian government has reduced excise duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre and exempted diesel from it to help oil marketing companies and reduce fuel costs.

India is prepared to face global challenges under the leadership of Prime Minister Modi, with ongoing monitoring of energy and supply chains to ensure uninterrupted availability, dismissing rumors of a lockdown.

The ongoing conflict in the West Asian region has caused significant economic impacts, including a weakening rupee and increased crude oil prices, leading to a 1% fall in stock markets.

Politics.summary:
Hagrama Mohilary, the head of the Bodoland Territorial Council, has joined hands with the BJP for the Assembly poll despite having a complicated history with the party. 

The Kerala High Court has directed the Chief Election Commission to consider a representation against BJP leader B.Gopalakrishnan over alleged communal remarks. 

The Kerala High Court has also held that the State Election Commission must issue a show-cause notice and afford an opportunity to file election expenses before disqualifying a candidate.

Finance.summary:
The Indian government has reduced excise duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre and exempted diesel from it to help oil marketing companies. 

The Reserve Bank of India has approved the voluntary amalgamation of The Bapunagar Mahila Co-operative Bank Ltd with Shri Vinayak Sahakari Bank Ltd. 

Major central banks have paused rate cuts due to the US-Iran war, affecting global monetary policy, particularly in India where the RBI is facing a weakening rupee and risks to inflation, growth, and the current account.

Technology.summary:
The Bombay High Court dismissed a petition seeking a CBI probe against Reliance Industries Limited and its director Mukesh Dhirubhai Ambani for allegedly stealing gas from ONGC fields. 

A Chennai court dismissed a plea by Pattali Makkal Katchi founder Dr S Ramadoss to freeze the party's mango symbol for the upcoming election, giving him liberty to approach the Election Commission.

Civil Liberties.summary:
The Madras High Court has temporarily stayed all further proceedings in a case against former Tamil Nadu Minister K Ponmudi, who is accused of making a hate speech. 

The Lok Sabha has passed a bill to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act 2019, which includes provisions for graded punishment based on the gravity of the offense. 

The Karnataka High Court is hearing a case regarding the menstrual leave policy, with women lawyers from Bengaluru defending the policy.

The Lok Sabha has passed the Finance Bill 2026-27, advancing the government's fiscal plans for the upcoming year.

Labour.summary:
The Cabinet has approved a ₹33,660 crore scheme to establish 100 new industrial parks by 2032, with the Central government providing funding of up to ₹1 crore per acre. 

The Karnataka High Court is considering a case filed by 15 women professionals who claim that a menstrual leave policy is discriminatory and creates a competitive disadvantage for women in hiring and promotions. 

The Calcutta High Court has closed a 22-year-old defamation case against a TV show that called CESC "Big Thief" and depicted its employees as Osama Bin Laden after the show's makers and anchor apologized unconditionally.

Urban.summary:
The Calcutta High Court has upheld the constitutional validity of a section of the Kolkata Municipal Corporation (Amendment) Act, 2022, which allows the revision of property valuation for levying property tax in a ₹11.24 crore dispute. 

The Gujarat High Court ruled that an electricity connection cannot be denied due to a co-sharer's lack of consent.

15 women professionals have filed a petition in the Karnataka High Court, arguing that a menstrual leave policy is "benevolent sexism" that creates a competitive disadvantage for women in hiring and promotions. 

Security has been increased in Odisha as large crowds of devotees visited temples on the occasion of Ram Navami. 

Fines alone may not be an effective way to regulate big tech companies, as inconsistent, immediate, and severe penalties are necessary to deter bad behavior.

Health.summary:
The Bombay High Court has cancelled the bail of an accused individual for supplying fake medicines to government hospitals, deeming it a serious threat to public health and eroding public trust in healthcare. 

India's government has cut fuel duty in a move seen as a timely measure to combat inflation and boost consumer demand amidst global turmoil, according to expert Deven Choksey. 

The Prime Minister is set to meet with Chief Ministers amid the Iran war, with the 'Team India' model from the COVID-19 crisis being revisited.

Environment.summary:

A conflict in West Asia involving Israel, Iran, and their allies has raised concerns about the potential impact on fertilizer availability in India, specifically urea supply.

The Kerala High Court has clarified that when issuing a notice for arrest, the exact quantity of seized contraband does not need to be stated if the nature of the contraband is mentioned.

Trilegal acted as the legal advisor for GSP Crop Science's ₹400 crore initial public offering. 

Prime Minister Modi reviewed fuel and fertilizer supplies to prevent black-marketing and hoarding amid the escalating West Asia conflict. 

Shivraj Singh Chouhan set up a cell to track the availability of seeds and fertilizers in India due to the West Asia crisis.

India has maintained its retail inflation target at 4 percent for the Reserve Bank of India for the next five years. 

The Indian stock market is likely to open in the red on Friday amid weak global cues, with Reliance Industries, LIC, and IREDA among the 10 stocks in focus.

A partnership between ICC Kerala and GETAI Foundation aims to offer free AI training for MSMEs and startups through a structured framework called AIfy Kerala.

Let’s examine the accuracy of this claim. Claim: The Ramayana was declared a fictional story in a 1971 Allahabad High Court case [...] The post The Allahabad High Court’s judgment on 19 January 1971 did not say that the Ramayana is a fictional story appeared first on FACTLY .
articles.raw_summary: A post circulating on social media (here, here, here)  claims that the Ramayana was declared a fictional story in a 1971 Allahabad High Court case (Case No. 412, verdict dated 19 January 1971). Let’s examine the accuracy of this claim. India retains inflation target at 4% for next five years
articles.summary: India has maintained its retail inflation target at 4 percent for the Reserve Bank of India. This decision ensures continuity and price stability amidst global economic uncertainties. The framework, set for another five years until March 2031, obligates the RBI to manage monetary policy for price control. This move influences government fiscal measures, aiming for overall economic stability.

This decision ensures continuity and price stability amidst global economic uncertainties. The framework, set for another five years until March 2031, obligates the RBI to manage monetary policy for price control. This move influences government fiscal measures, aiming for overall economic stability.

articles.title: Lok Sabha passes Finance Bill 2026–27, advancing government’s fiscal plans

articles.title: India retains headline inflation target at 4% following review
articles.summary: India's government has kept the retail inflation target at 4 percent. This target falls within a 2 to 6 percent band. The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee is responsible for meeting this goal. This inflation targeting framework was adopted in 2016 and last reviewed in 2021. The committee comprises central bank and government appointees.

articles.summary: India has reduced its fuel duty, lowering the special additional excise duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre and removing it entirely on diesel. articles.title: WWD 2026: Collective Groundwater Conservation Efforts in India
articles.summary: India is the world’s largest user of groundwater, with over 21.9 million wells and tubewells extracting an estimated 247 billion cubic metres annually. This invisible resource supports most of the country’s drinking water needs and sustains the livelihoods of millions of small and marginal farmers. Yet despite its centrality to India’s water security, groundwater has long been poorly understood and weakly governed.

articles.title: What insurance numbers do not reveal
articles.summary: The article examines how India's insurance penetration and density metrics—widely used to gauge insurance coverage—only reflect premium collection and not whether households are adequately protected against income loss. It argues that rapid premium growth is often misinterpreted as progress, even when financial safeguards remain weak. The analysis suggests current measures fail to capture the true effectiveness of insurance in shielding people from economic shocks.
articles.raw_summary: Insurance penetration and density are widely used measures, but they show premium collection, not how well households are protected against loss of income; as a result, premium growth is often mistaken for progress, even when financial protection remains limited

articles.summary: The West Asia war could slash India’s fertilizer production by 15% and hike the government’s subsidy burden by up to ₹25,000 crore. While 70% gas allocation to urea plants and current inventories provide a buffer, prolonged disruption threatens manufacturer profitability and fiscal targets.

articles.summary: The article explores divided opinions on AI's impact on jobs in India, with some predicting job losses and others foreseeing entirely new employment opportunities. It suggests the reality may lie between these extremes. The discussion is relevant to India's evolving workforce policies and technology-driven economic shifts.

The pact aims to avoid vote-splitting among opposition parties.

articles.raw_summary: In a notarised document Friday, Vijayamma stated that her late husband YSR’s wealth was never fairly apportioned to daughter Sharmila and her four grandchildren.

The decision could impact global oil supply and prices, with potential implications for India's energy security and import strategies.

The debate highlights differing perspectives on how to best communicate health risks to consumers.

articles.summary: The digital payments industry has received about Rs 8,000 crore in subsidy payments over four years for UPI and RuPay debit card payments, against a higher allocation. The government removed MDR on these payments, and the fintech industry is yet to receive FY26 payouts, amid concerns over costs, slowdown and future growth.

articles.summary: Google will now label verified investment apps on its Indian app store. This move aims to help users identify genuine trading platforms and prevent financial fraud. Only brokers and intermediaries registered with SEBI will receive this verified badge. This initiative is a response to the recent rise in online financial scams.

articles.summary: Swiggy is launching voice ordering for food and groceries. Users can now order and book tables by speaking in their preferred Indian language. This new feature uses Sarvam's AI assistant, Indus. The integration covers Swiggy, Instamart, and DineOut. Razorpay is also part of the end-to-end process. This innovation aims to make ordering easier for millions across India.

articles.title: A third of Indian PC users faced offline cyber attack in 2025; over 6.4 crore incidents blocked: Kaspersky
articles.summary: In 2025, almost one in three Indian computer users faced local attacks. These attacks often came from USB drives. Hackers are also using fake versions of popular tools like Microsoft Teams. They aim to steal login details and banking information. This highlights the ongoing risks for computer users.

articles.title: India’s 2026 wheat output seen higher, but misses estimates after weather hit
articles.summary: Indias wheat harvest in 2026 is projected to increase from the previous year. However, unseasonal rains and hailstorms have impacted the maturing crop. This has led to a downward revision of initial production estimates. Despite these challenges, the harvest is still expected to surpass last year's output. Quality concerns may arise in some northern regions.

articles.title: Reserve Bank of India to hold interest rates until at least mid-2027: Reuters poll
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will keep its key interest rate at 5.25%. This decision is expected to hold until mid-2027. Xu emphasized the importance of good-neighborly relations and mutual success for both nations.

articles.title: CSR spends up but aspirational districts fail to get bigger share
articles.summary: Corporate social responsibility spending by Indian companies has surged significantly over the last decade. However, a recent study reveals that aspirational districts, facing developmental challenges, have received only a small fraction of this investment. While over ₹1.22 lakh crore has been spent on CSR, just 12% reached these targeted areas, indicating an uneven distribution of funds.

articles.title: Sugar feels the heat as eateries cook less
articles.summary: India's sugar sales are down this month. Restaurants and hotels are cooking less because of LPG supply issues. This means sugar mills are not meeting their government-set sales quotas. March usually sees lower sugar demand after the holiday season. Global sugar exports are also slow due to market issues and higher costs.

articles.title: At WTO meet, Piyush Goyal says plurilateral agreements must not be imposed
articles.summary: India urged consensus on WTO plurilateral outcomes and careful reconsideration of the e-commerce duty moratorium. The US wants the moratorium made permanent. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal also called for restoring the dispute settlement system and effective special and differential treatment. Discussions are ongoing at the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference in Cameroon.

articles.title: Gulf conflict puts India's FY27 growth at risk
articles.summary: India's economy faces pressure from the West Asia conflict. Economists predict a growth slowdown for FY27. Higher crude oil prices and supply issues are impacting stability. Several key sectors are expected to be affected. Forecasts for India's economic growth have been revised downwards by multiple agencies. A prolonged conflict could significantly alter growth and inflation.

articles.title: OECD lowers India’s FY27 growth outlook to 6.1% amid global uncertainty
articles.summary: The OECD has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.1% due to global uncertainty and rising energy prices, despite expected support from reduced US tariffs. Inflation is projected to rise sharply to 5.1% in FY27, prompting the OECD to anticipate a temporary policy rate hike in India during Q2 2026.

articles.summary: India has launched an investigation into paperboard imports from China and Indonesia. The probe follows allegations that subsidies provided by these countries are harming Indian manufacturers. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies will determine if these imports are causing injury.

articles.title: West Asia turmoil to swell fertiliser subsidy by up to Rs 25,000 Crore, hit output: Crisil Ratings
articles.summary: India faces a potential Rs 20,000-25,000 crore increase in its fertiliser subsidy bill. Domestic production of fertilisers may also fall by 10-15 percent. These impacts stem from ongoing Middle East conflicts disrupting raw material and fertiliser imports. The government's allocation of gas and existing inventory offer some buffer. However, the industry will need additional subsidy support.

articles.title: Rupee slide may fuel imported inflation risks, say experts
articles.summary: The Indian rupee is weakening, raising concerns about rising imported inflation. Higher global oil and commodity prices, coupled with increased logistics costs due to the Gulf conflict, are being passed on to consumers. Economists warn that continued conflict and elevated prices will add to future inflationary pressures. Businesses are already increasing prices as input costs rise.

articles.title: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman moves Bill to amend IBC, speed up resolution
articles.summary: A new bill aims to speed up bankruptcy resolution in India. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman introduced amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. The proposed changes focus on creditor-led, out-of-court settlements. This aims to reduce delays and improve the process for resolving insolvent companies. The bill also addresses cross-border and corporate group insolvency cases.

articles.title: Infra projects see cost overrun of Rs 5.66 lakh cr in Feb

Transport and Logistics leads with 1,421 projects. 9 projects were commissioned in February 2026.

articles.title: India backs WTO reset with core focus intact
articles.summary: India advocates for World Trade Organisation reform, emphasizing development for poorer nations and consensus-based decisions. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stressed the need to revive the dispute settlement mechanism and address long-pending issues like public stockholding for cotton-growing countries. India also signaled reservations about incorporating plurilateral agreements and continuing customs waivers for e-commerce.
